eligibility: Indigenous organizations, communities, First Nations (bands/councils), Tribal councils, Inuit organizations, Indigenous-owned businesses
funding_amount: Up to 75% of project costs (max $2M for Indigenous organizations); $22,500-$150,000 per charger depending on output
application_deadline: Indigenous stream currently closed; General stream open until funding committed
description: Provides funding for EV charger deployment in public places, workplaces, multi-unit residential buildings, and fleets. Indigenous organizations receive enhanced funding at 75% vs 50% for non-Indigenous applicants.

grant_title: Canada Infrastructure Bank Indigenous Community Infrastructure Initiative (ICII)
eligibility: First Nation, Métis and Inuit communities and their economic development corporations
funding_amount: Low-interest loans minimum $5M, up to 80% of project costs; $1B+ committed to Indigenous projects
description: Low-cost, long-term financing for revenue-generating infrastructure projects across 5 priority sectors: clean power, green infrastructure, public transit, broadband, trade and transportation.

grant_title: Canada Infrastructure Bank Indigenous Equity Initiative (IEI)
eligibility: First Nation, Métis and Inuit communities seeking equity stakes in infrastructure projects
funding_amount: Loans for equity purchases in projects where CIB is also investing
description: Provides loans to Indigenous communities to support them in purchasing equity ownership stakes in infrastructure projects within their traditional territories.

grant_title: Wah-ila-toos / Clean Energy for Rural and Remote Communities (CERRC)
eligibility: First Nations, Inuit, Métis communities, Indigenous governments, tribal councils, development corporations, Indigenous organizations
funding_amount: Up to $5M per project; $453M program over 8 years
application_deadline: Ongoing (some streams on waitlist due to limited funding)
description: Supports clean energy projects (wind, solar, hydro, biomass, geothermal) to reduce diesel reliance in Indigenous, rural and remote communities. Single-window approach through Wah-ila-toos hub.

administering_agency: Crown-Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Canada (CIRNAC)
eligibility: Northern and Indigenous communities transitioning to renewable energy
funding_amount: $300M over 5 years; $40.4M for hydroelectricity feasibility/planning
description: Supports northern and Indigenous communities in transitioning to renewable, sustainable and affordable energy sources. Part of Wah-ila-toos initiative.

grant_title: Indigenous Leadership Fund (Low Carbon Economy Fund)
eligibility: First Nations, Inuit, Métis governments, communities, organizations (must be 51%+ Indigenous-owned)
funding_amount: Up to $180M by 2029; First Nations stream up to $6M/project; Designated stream up to $1M/project
application_deadline: First Nations and Designated streams currently closed; Métis stream remains open
description: Funds Indigenous-owned and led renewable energy, energy efficiency, and low-carbon heating projects. Projects must be past concept stage and ready for deployment.

grant_title: Smart Renewables and Electrification Pathways Program - Indigenous-Led Clean Energy Stream
eligibility: Indigenous-led projects demonstrating meaningful Indigenous ownership/participation
funding_amount: $4.5B total program; variable per project based on scope
application_deadline: Spring 2025 RFP closed; future intakes expected
description: Supports Indigenous-led clean electricity deployment (wind, solar, hydro, battery storage) and grid modernization projects with direct community benefits.

administering_agency: Crown-Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Canada (CIRNAC)
eligibility: First Nation communities and organizations south of 60th parallel
funding_amount: Variable; dedicated flood mapping and water resiliency funding available through 2025-26
description: Supports climate change adaptation projects including risk assessments, flood mapping, small-scale implementation of adaptation actions for flooding, wildfires, drought, sea level rise.

eligibility: First Nations, Inuit, Métis Nations, communities and organizations
funding_amount: Up to $76.9M over 10 years for Indigenous stream
description: Supports ecological restoration, improved land management, and conservation activities that sequester carbon while benefiting biodiversity, climate resiliency, and human well-being.

eligibility: First Nation and Inuit communities and their governments, tribal councils
funding_amount: Up to $250K (Regional Process); larger projects through National Prioritization Framework; 30-80% of project costs
application_deadline: 2025-26 intake reopened November 1, 2025, runs until March 31, 2026
description: Provides funding for economic opportunities, business planning, advisory services, commercial development, and equity gap financing for community-owned businesses.

eligibility: Indigenous businesses, organizations, communities, local governments (through federal partner referral)
funding_amount: $14.45M annual budget + $12M annually for clean energy until 2027
application_deadline: Ongoing (recipients cannot apply directly; identified through federal partners)
description: Horizontal program coordinating federal investments in Indigenous economic development across multiple departments. Additional funding for clean energy initiatives.

grant_title: Aboriginal Entrepreneurship Program - Access to Capital
eligibility: Indigenous entrepreneurs through Indigenous Financial Institutions (IFIs) and Métis Capital Corporations
funding_amount: $514M over 10 years (2024-2034); non-repayable equity contributions for businesses
description: Promotes Indigenous entrepreneurship by providing access to capital and business services through network of Indigenous Financial Institutions to start, expand or acquire businesses.

eligibility: First Nations governments, Indigenous-owned entities, partners working with Indigenous communities
funding_amount: Up to $10M per recipient per year for capital projects
application_deadline: FNIIP submission deadline in fall each year; no deadline for funding
description: Supports planning and skills development, solid waste management, roads and bridges, energy systems, connectivity, fire protection, cultural/recreational facilities, band administrative buildings.

grant_title: Green and Inclusive Community Buildings Program (GICB)
eligibility: Indigenous governing bodies, band councils, Indigenous not-for-profits, Indigenous development corporations
funding_amount: Up to $25M; Indigenous projects can receive up to 100% funding; At least 10% of program funding for Indigenous recipients
description: Supports green retrofits and new construction of community buildings to reduce GHG emissions and improve accessibility. Indigenous and territorial projects receive enhanced funding.

eligibility: Indigenous governing bodies, band councils, Indigenous development corporations, not-for-profits with Indigenous mandate
funding_amount: $1B+ available; minimum $138M allocated to Indigenous recipients
description: Invests in structural and natural infrastructure to increase community resilience against natural disasters triggered by climate change.

grant_title: Indigenous Skills and Employment Training (ISET) Program
eligibility: Indigenous people seeking skills training and employment through Indigenous service delivery organizations
funding_amount: Variable; funding for tuition, books, training supports, living expenses
application_deadline: Ongoing through Indigenous service delivery organizations
description: Helps Indigenous people improve skills and find employment through distinctions-based approach for First Nations, Métis and Inuit.

eligibility: Indigenous communities, Indigenous-owned businesses (majority ownership), municipalities, broader public sector
funding_amount: Up to $1M per project; Indigenous communities eligible for up to 75% of project costs
description: Provides funding for public EV charger installation. Indigenous communities and Northern Ontario projects receive enhanced 75% funding rate.

eligibility: First Nation and Métis communities and organizations in Ontario
funding_amount: $25M provincial funding; $500K/year per remote First Nation for diesel reduction
description: Supports capacity building, community energy planning, clean energy project development, feasibility studies, and energy resiliency initiatives.

eligibility: Indigenous entrepreneurs, businesses, First Nation and Métis communities, Indigenous organizations
funding_amount: $70M over 7 years; grants through Economic Diversification Grant, Business and Community Fund, Regional Partnership Grant
description: Supports economic diversification, business start-up/expansion, employment and training, regional partnerships for Indigenous economic development.

eligibility: First Nations for equity ownership in energy projects (transmission, renewable energy)
funding_amount: Government-backed financing; $1.34M minimum loan guarantee
description: Provides government-backed financing for First Nations equity ownership in new transmission or renewable energy projects (wind, solar, hydroelectric).

grant_title: First Nations Clean Energy Business Fund (FNCEBF)
administering_agency: BC Ministry of Energy and Climate Solutions
eligibility: BC First Nations (bands under Indian Act or governing bodies representing one or more First Nations)
funding_amount: Capacity: up to $50K; Equity: up to $500K per applicant ($150K per project); plus revenue sharing agreements
application_deadline: Next intake early 2026; April 30, 2025 deadline passed
description: Supports clean energy feasibility studies, community energy planning, equity positions in clean energy projects, and revenue sharing from clean energy projects.

administering_agency: New Relationship Trust / Pacific Economic Development Canada
eligibility: First Nations throughout BC for clean energy projects
funding_amount: $9.8M (federal/provincial combined) in 2024; project-specific amounts vary
description: Provides support and capacity-building funds for Indigenous clean energy projects including pre-construction, feasibility, engineering, and deployment.

eligibility: Indigenous communities and Indigenous housing providers in BC Hydro/FortisBC service territory
funding_amount: Free energy-saving products, salary funding for training, rebates for home energy upgrades
description: Provides free energy-saving products, training stipends, and rebates for home energy efficiency upgrades including insulation, windows, heating systems.

eligibility: Indigenous communities for investments in natural resources, agriculture, telecommunications, transportation, tourism, healthcare, technology
funding_amount: $3B loan guarantee program; minimum $20M Indigenous investment per project; max $250M guarantee per investment
description: Provides loan guarantees to increase access to capital and improve lending terms for Indigenous investments in major projects across multiple sectors.

eligibility: First Nations, Métis Settlements, Métis and non-status Indigenous communities, Indigenous-owned businesses in Alberta
funding_amount: Variable; supports capacity building to project completion
description: Supports renewable energy generation, energy efficiency improvements, capacity building, community energy planning, feasibility studies for Indigenous communities.

eligibility: Indigenous communities or corporate entities 51%+ owned by Indigenous communities in Alberta
funding_amount: $150,000 to $750,000 per project
application_deadline: One application per community per funding year
description: Helps Indigenous community-owned businesses capitalize on business development opportunities to improve socio-economic outcomes.

grant_title: Northern and Regional Economic Development Program (NRED)
eligibility: Indigenous communities in Alberta; up to 75% for Indigenous applicants
funding_amount: Up to 75% of eligible costs (min $13,333 project); grants range $25K-$300K
description: Supports economic development and diversification projects including feasibility studies, business plans, tourism development, small business incubators.

eligibility: Indigenous communities, businesses, entrepreneurs, organizations in Quebec
funding_amount: Variable by component (Economic Development, Community Infrastructure, Loan Guarantees, Social Development, etc.)
description: Seven-component program supporting economic development, community infrastructure, loan guarantees, community action, social development, consultation support, and urban Indigenous services.

eligibility: Indigenous business owners and entrepreneurs in Manitoba
funding_amount: $4M initial investment; grants for programs and services
description: Supports Indigenous economic reconciliation and inclusion through grants for Indigenous business owners and entrepreneurs.

eligibility: First Nations businesses in Manitoba
funding_amount: Variable; financial support for business development
description: Provides financial support to First Nations businesses for startup, expansion, and acquisition.

eligibility: First Nation entrepreneurs and businesses in Saskatchewan
funding_amount: Conditional grants; Indigenous Women Entrepreneur (IWE) program available
description: Needs-based conditional grant program for First Nation entrepreneurs and businesses. Includes dedicated Indigenous Women Entrepreneur program.

grant_title: First Nations and Métis Community Partnership Projects
eligibility: First Nations Band/Tribal Councils, Métis Nation, Aboriginal non-profits, municipalities partnering with Indigenous communities
funding_amount: Up to $45,000 per project
description: Supports innovative partnership projects in safe communities, strong families, student achievement, and economic growth categories.
